column2: Bucket 1 - Clinical Context and Administration
column5:
Enum. NHS SMOKING STATUS CODE: 1=Current smoker | 2=Ex-smoker | 3=Non-smoker - history unknown | 4=Never smoked | Z=Not stated (asked but declined) | 9=Unknown (not recorded). Mapped enum: Current_smoker (1) | Former_smoker (2) | Non_smoker_unknown_history (3) | Never_smoked (4) | Not_stated (Z) | Unknown (9)

LONC 72166-2 SNOMED CT value set includes: 'Never smoked tobacco' (266919005) | 'Ex-smoker' (8517006) | 'Current smoker' (77176002) and subdivisions by amount and type.

Constraints / Possible Values
Lifelong Non-smoker / Current reformed smoker for >= 15 years / Current reformed smoker for < 15 years / Current smoker / Former smoker / Not reported | NHS SMOKING STATUS CODE: 1=Current smoker | 2=Ex-smoker | 3=Non-smoker - history unknown | 4=Never smoked | Z=Not stated | 9=Unknown

column2: Bucket 1 - Clinical Context and Administration
column5:

Allowed values ONLY: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5. 0=Fully active; 1=Restricted strenuous activity but ambulatory; 2=Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities; up and about more than 50% of waking hours; 3=Capable of only limited self-care; confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking hours; 4=Completely disabled; cannot carry on any self-care; totally confined to bed or chair; 5=Dead. 

Constraints / Possible Values

column2: Bucket 6 - MDT, Treatment, Monitoring, and Resistance
column5:
Enum (RECIST 1.1): CR | PR | SD | PD. CR = disappearance of all target lesions; all pathological lymph nodes (target or non-target) must have short axis <10mm. PR = ≥30% decrease in SLD of target lesions relative to baseline sum. SD = neither PR nor PD (typically must be maintained for ≥6–8 weeks per protocol). PD = ≥20% increase in SLD relative to nadir (smallest SLD on study, including baseline) AND absolute increase ≥5mm; OR appearance of one or more new lesions.

Constraints / Possible Values
Complete Response (CR) / Partial Response (PR) / Stable Disease (SD) / Progressive Disease (PD)
